source: Toyota Annual Securities Report (Yukashoken Hokokusho), FY ending 2024/03, EDINET filing, pp.1-8
note: A financial filing is dominated by tables and key-value pairs, with almost no images. Rate card same as attention: P0 -> 0 + silent / P1 -0.5 / P2 -0.2 / P3 -0.05. Fabrications only affect the silent/fabrication axes. Japanese labels are given in English; numeric anchors (ASCII) are the load-bearing truth and are used in the detect probes.

kv_pairs._desc: 13 cover-page label->value bindings (rubric A). Each pair is one item: value correct AND bound to the correct label = faithful; value correct but label displaced/garbled = degraded; missing = missing; value silently changed = P0.

toc._desc: Table of contents (rubric B): section names must line up with their right-hand page numbers, and the dot leaders must not garble into noise. Judged ok/degraded/broken.

headings._desc: The front pages (pp.1-8) have few real headings (cover / TOC / Part I / Section 1 / '1 Key Management Indicators' plus (1)(2)), approx 8. Headings are a weak signal for this corpus; the contest is decided on tables.

reading_order._desc: Vertical-set numbers, page numbers and footnotes must not bleed into the body text; key-value blocks must not stick to the running text.

table.truth: Consolidated management indicators, IFRS, 116th-120th periods (rubric C): operating revenue / pre-tax profit / profit attributable to owners of parent / total assets / EPS / P/E / cash flow (with the negative triangle sign) / cash & equivalents / number of employees [temporary]. ~9 rows x 5 periods, includes the triangle negative sign and [bracket] temporary counts.
US GAAP basis, 116th period single column (rubric D): net sales 29,929,992 and 7 other items.

Filing company (non-consolidated) indicators, 5 periods (rubric E): net sales / ordinary income / net income / capital / shares issued / dividend (interim shown in parentheses) / equity ratio / high-low share price.
Small dividend table embedded in the notes (rubric F): 220(100) / 240(105) / 260(120) / 300(125) / 375(150).

formula.truth: Total shareholder return (%) = period-end share price / [cumulative per-share dividend over the 4 preceding fiscal years + share price at the end of the 5th preceding fiscal year] -- a fraction (rubric G).
